title: Print a Fruit List
description: Create a list called **fruits** with the values `'apple'`, `'banana'`, `'cherry'`. Then use a **for loop** to print each fruit on its own line.
starter_code: # Create your list and loop here

code_requirements.description: Creates a variable called 'fruits' with a list containing 'apple', 'banana', 'cherry'
code_requirements.fail_message: Create a variable called 'fruits' and assign it a list with the items 'apple', 'banana', 'cherry'.

code_requirements.description: Uses a 'for' loop to iterate over the list
code_requirements.fail_message: Use a 'for' loop to iterate through the list and print each fruit instead of printing them one by one.

hints: Create a list with square brackets: fruits = ['apple', 'banana', 'cherry']
Use a for loop: for fruit in fruits:
Inside the loop, call print(fruit) on each item
